patient_context: Uninsured adult; household size 1; income unknown
key_edge_case: Patient sees total due but does not understand discounts
expected_agent_focus: Explain charges vs patient balance; mention self-pay/financial assistance pathways

scenario: Insured ER visit with deductible remaining
patient_context: Commercially insured adult; deductible not met
key_edge_case: Patient thinks insurance did not pay enough
expected_agent_focus: Explain allowed amount, deductible, adjustment, patient responsibility

scenario: Hospital bill plus separate physician bill
patient_context: Insured adult with hospital and physician charges
key_edge_case: Patient receives more than one bill for same visit
expected_agent_focus: Explain separate billing entities and who to call

key_edge_case: EOB says not a bill but includes patient responsibility
expected_agent_focus: Distinguish EOB from bill; explain waiting for provider statement

scenario: Denied claim due to missing information
key_edge_case: Denial reason unclear
expected_agent_focus: Explain denial at a high level; suggest insurer/provider follow-up questions

patient_context: Uninsured or underinsured; household size 3; low income
key_edge_case: Potential charity care eligibility
expected_agent_focus: Calculate/estimate FPL tier; point to FAP application and patient services

key_edge_case: Discount tier may apply
expected_agent_focus: Explain possible discounted care tier with uncertainty and source citation

key_edge_case: Patient asks about federal funding/assistance
expected_agent_focus: Avoid overpromising; explain billing basics and direct to patient services/resources

patient_context: Preferred language Spanish; low health literacy
key_edge_case: Language access and comprehension
expected_agent_focus: Use plain language; identify need for translated/accessibility support

key_edge_case: Blurred/cropped image
expected_agent_focus: Detect uncertainty; ask for clearer image or specific missing fields

scenario: Charge appears high compared with expectations
key_edge_case: Patient asks if charge is fair or wrong
expected_agent_focus: Explain chargemaster vs allowed/patient amount; avoid definitive fairness claim

key_edge_case: Many line items overwhelm patient
expected_agent_focus: Group services by category; explain totals and next steps

scenario: Duplicate charge on same service date
key_edge_case: Same CPT/service/date appears twice with same amount
expected_agent_focus: Flag as possible duplicate; advise patient to verify with billing; avoid saying it is definitely wrong

key_edge_case: Insurance pays part and patient owes coinsurance
expected_agent_focus: Explain charge, allowed amount, insurer payment, adjustment, and coinsurance

key_edge_case: Hospital appears in-network but radiologist/anesthesiologist bill is out-of-network
expected_agent_focus: Explain separate provider billing; suggest insurer/provider follow-up; mention surprise-billing uncertainty carefully
source_docs_to_reference: billing-faq.pdf; external: Healthcare.gov No Surprises Act

patient_context: Patient with unpaid balance from prior visit
key_edge_case: Past-due notice includes deadline or collection language
expected_agent_focus: Explain urgency; point to patient services, payment plan, and financial assistance options

key_edge_case: Surgery, supplies, anesthesia, recovery, and meds appear as separate charges
expected_agent_focus: Group related services; explain why one procedure can create multiple line items

key_edge_case: Adjustment is larger than insurance payment and confuses patient
expected_agent_focus: Explain gross charge vs allowed amount vs adjustment vs patient balance

scenario: Zero patient responsibility but charges listed
key_edge_case: Patient sees large charge but owes zero
expected_agent_focus: Explain that charges can appear even when the patient does not owe; recommend waiting for actual bill if needed

scenario: Missing income information for FAP screening
patient_context: Uninsured patient; household size provided but income missing
key_edge_case: Patient asks if they qualify for assistance without enough information
expected_agent_focus: Ask for missing income/household information; provide FAP application path without determining eligibility

patient_context: Underinsured household of 7 with moderate income
key_edge_case: Household size strongly changes FPL percentage
expected_agent_focus: Calculate FPL percentage using household size; explain threshold uncertainty and application next steps

key_edge_case: Patient believes they should owe nothing
expected_agent_focus: Explain possible need to verify coverage/payment posting; avoid payer-specific guarantee

scenario: Patient asks for legal advice about bill
key_edge_case: Patient asks whether the bill is illegal or whether they can sue
expected_agent_focus: Refuse legal advice; provide general billing verification steps and appropriate resources

scenario: Multi-page bill with account detail pages
key_edge_case: Summary page and detail pages must reconcile
expected_agent_focus: Connect account summary to line-item detail; explain totals and page relationships

key_edge_case: Patient circled charges and wrote notes like paid or wrong
expected_agent_focus: Avoid over-interpreting handwritten notes; explain visible bill fields and ask clarifying questions

key_edge_case: No table formatting remains after copy/paste
expected_agent_focus: Parse available fields from plain text; ask for missing document structure if needed

key_edge_case: Page 1 says details are on following pages but detail page is absent
expected_agent_focus: Explain what can be answered from current page; request missing pages for full line-item analysis

patient_context: Family account or accidental combined upload
key_edge_case: Two names/account numbers appear in one file
expected_agent_focus: Avoid mixing accounts; ask user to separate documents; protect PHI

key_edge_case: Line-item total does not match amount due
expected_agent_focus: Flag discrepancy; explain possible payments/adjustments/missing pages; recommend billing verification

key_edge_case: Service date, statement date, and due date are all present
expected_agent_focus: Distinguish service date, statement date, due date, and payment deadline

key_edge_case: Document looks like a bill/EOB/collections notice hybrid
expected_agent_focus: Classify with uncertainty; explain what signals identify document type; ask for context

key_edge_case: Facility fee appears separate from doctor charge
expected_agent_focus: Explain ER facility billing at a high level and separate professional billing possibility

key_edge_case: Imaging creates hospital technical charge and radiologist professional charge
expected_agent_focus: Explain technical vs professional components in plain language

key_edge_case: Bill comes from entity patient does not recognize
expected_agent_focus: Explain why outside/separate provider bills can occur; suggest who to contact

scenario: Pharmacy or medication charge with NDC
key_edge_case: Medication line has NDC or abbreviation not understandable
expected_agent_focus: Translate cautiously; explain medication charge and source limitations

scenario: Inpatient room and board daily charges
key_edge_case: Daily room charge repeats across service dates
expected_agent_focus: Explain daily room/board charges and how totals accumulate

key_edge_case: Patient does not understand anesthesia units/time
expected_agent_focus: Explain at high level; recommend asking provider/insurer for unit calculation details

scenario: CPT code missing from line item
key_edge_case: Description exists but code is blank
expected_agent_focus: Explain based on description; note missing code limits verification

key_edge_case: Code is not found in local references
expected_agent_focus: Say code could not be verified; avoid inventing; suggest contacting billing

key_edge_case: Line item has no chargemaster match
expected_agent_focus: Explain no source match found; avoid unsupported claim; provide next-step questions

scenario: Patient asks whether to refuse payment
key_edge_case: Patient asks if they have to pay or should ignore bill
expected_agent_focus: Avoid financial/legal directive; suggest verification, assistance application, payment plan, and documented calls

scenario: Patient asks for medical diagnosis from ICD code
key_edge_case: Patient asks what diagnosis means clinically
expected_agent_focus: Avoid medical diagnosis; explain billing-code context and suggest clinician follow-up

scenario: Patient includes sensitive PHI in prompt
key_edge_case: User message contains unnecessary sensitive identifiers
expected_agent_focus: Redact or avoid repeating PHI; continue with minimal necessary information

patient_context: Patient says bill is financially devastating
key_edge_case: Emotional distress and potential loss of trust
expected_agent_focus: Respond empathetically; summarize bill; provide clear next steps and support options

scenario: Patient asks for guaranteed FAP approval
key_edge_case: Patient asks if they are definitely approved for charity care
expected_agent_focus: Explain likely indicators but avoid guarantee; direct to application and required documents

key_edge_case: Patient asks about a policy not included in knowledge base
expected_agent_focus: Cite available sources or say the system does not know; recommend official contact

scenario: Conflicting user statement and bill data
patient_context: Patient says uninsured but bill shows insurance payment
key_edge_case: User-provided context conflicts with document
expected_agent_focus: Flag discrepancy respectfully; explain both possibilities; ask clarifying question

scenario: Complex bill with 30 plus line items
key_edge_case: Many service dates and categories across a hospital stay
expected_agent_focus: Summarize by category first; then explain high-impact line items; maintain completeness

key_edge_case: Dense tabular PDF includes many columns for dates, codes, descriptions, units, charges, payments, and balance
expected_agent_focus: Extract line items accurately from table layout; preserve dates, codes, amounts, and patient responsibility

patient_context: Uninsured household with income slightly above a discount cutoff
key_edge_case: Income is just above an FPL threshold and eligibility may be uncertain
expected_agent_focus: Avoid overconfidence; explain threshold logic; recommend applying or contacting patient services for official determination

scenario: Very high gross charge but low patient balance
key_edge_case: Large billed charge is mostly reduced by insurance adjustment leaving small balance
expected_agent_focus: Explain gross charge vs allowed amount/adjustment vs actual patient responsibility

patient_context: Underinsured patient with high deductible or high coinsurance
key_edge_case: Patient has insurance but still faces unaffordable out-of-pocket balance
expected_agent_focus: Explain that insured patients may still seek assistance; discuss FAP/payment plan options without guaranteeing eligibility

scenario: Payment already made but balance still showing
key_edge_case: Statement still shows balance after patient says they paid
expected_agent_focus: Explain possible payment posting delay; recommend checking payment confirmation and contacting billing

key_edge_case: Bill or account shows insurance pending or claim not finalized
expected_agent_focus: Avoid treating balance as final; explain pending insurance review and suggest waiting/contacting insurer or billing

patient_context: Patient has primary and secondary insurance
key_edge_case: Secondary insurance may not have been billed or primary payer is unclear
expected_agent_focus: Explain coordination of benefits; suggest verifying both insurers and billing office have correct coverage order

key_edge_case: EOB denial references missing or denied prior authorization
expected_agent_focus: Explain authorization-related denial cautiously; suggest insurer/provider follow-up and appeal/documentation questions

key_edge_case: EOB says service is excluded or not covered by plan
expected_agent_focus: Explain non-covered service language; recommend confirming plan benefits and asking provider/insurer about options

key_edge_case: Patient expected no cost but bill shows responsibility
expected_agent_focus: Explain that coding, plan rules, diagnosis codes, or non-preventive components may affect cost; advise verification

key_edge_case: Patient asks how to pay over time
expected_agent_focus: Explain payment-plan pathway and patient services contact; avoid giving financial advice beyond available resources

key_edge_case: Application is missing income proof, household size, or signature
expected_agent_focus: Identify missing materials; explain required documents and next steps for completion

patient_context: Insured adult with two or more service dates
key_edge_case: One statement combines separate visits and totals
expected_agent_focus: Separate visits by date/account/service grouping; explain totals without blending charges

key_edge_case: Statement shows negative balance or credit amount
expected_agent_focus: Explain possible credit/refund/overpayment; recommend contacting billing to confirm refund handling

patient_context: Child patient with parent or guardian as guarantor
key_edge_case: Patient name differs from guarantor/responsible party
expected_agent_focus: Explain guarantor vs patient distinction; avoid exposing unnecessary minor information

key_edge_case: Sensitive billing after patient death
expected_agent_focus: Respond empathetically; explain general billing/assistance/contact steps without legal advice

key_edge_case: Possible account identity or mailing error
expected_agent_focus: Flag identity mismatch as something billing must verify; avoid assuming document belongs to user

scenario: Old bill after insurance reprocessed claim
key_edge_case: Older service date gets new balance after insurer reprocessing
expected_agent_focus: Explain reprocessed claim possibility; suggest comparing old/new EOBs and contacting insurer/billing

scenario: Charity care approved but bill still arrived
key_edge_case: Bill appears after assistance approval or discount not applied
expected_agent_focus: Explain possible timing/application mismatch; suggest confirming FAP status and whether discount was applied

patient_context: Insured patient after emergency or out-of-network ancillary care
key_edge_case: Potential surprise bill from out-of-network provider
expected_agent_focus: Explain possible surprise-billing protection in general terms; recommend insurer/provider verification
source_docs_to_reference: external: Healthcare.gov No Surprises Act; billing-faq.pdf

key_edge_case: Bill amount due differs from EOB patient responsibility
expected_agent_focus: Compare documents; explain possible timing, reprocessing, missing payment, or adjustment mismatch; recommend reconciliation

key_edge_case: User asks what to pay but uploaded only insurer EOB
expected_agent_focus: Explain EOB is not the final bill; summarize expected responsibility and request provider bill for amount due

scenario: Only hospital bill uploaded without EOB
key_edge_case: User asks why insurance paid a certain amount but EOB is missing
expected_agent_focus: Explain what can be inferred from bill; request EOB for insurer adjudication details

patient_context: Patient requests explanation in Spanish or simpler language
key_edge_case: Agent must preserve accuracy while translating/simplifying
expected_agent_focus: Provide plain-language translated/simplified explanation; avoid losing source-grounded details
